title: Swiss Patent Office
description: Einstein began working at the Swiss Patent Office in Bern, where he developed his special theory of relativity.

title: Annus Mirabilis
description: Einstein published four groundbreaking papers outlining the theory of the photoelectric effect, Brownian motion, special relativity, and mass-energy equivalence.

title: Departure from Patent Office
description: Conclusion of his tenure at the Swiss Patent Office in Bern.

title: General Relativity
description: Published a paper on general relativity incorporating his theory of gravitation.

title: Stimulated Emission of Light
description: Published a paper on the quantum theory of radiation and stimulated emission of light, laying the foundation for the laser.

title: Nobel Prize in Physics
description: Awarded the Nobel Prize for his services to theoretical physics, specifically for the discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect.

title: Migration to the United States
description: While visiting the US, Adolf Hitler came to power in Germany; Einstein decided to remain in the US to escape Nazi persecution.

title: American Citizenship
description: Einstein was granted citizenship of the United States.

title: Letter to President Roosevelt
description: On the eve of WWII, he endorsed a letter to President Franklin D. Roosevelt alerting him to the potential development of powerful new bombs, which led to the Manhattan Project.

title: Death
description: Einstein passed away while affiliated with the Institute for Advanced Study in Princeton, New Jersey.
